protocol_description: Chainlink Payment Abstraction V2 is an auction-based system for converting accumulated fee tokens (ERC-20) into LINK via Dutch auction and CowSwap solver integration. The protocol manages concurrent auctions per asset with configurable parameters, price feeds from Chainlink Data Streams, and Chainlink Automation-driven lifecycle management.
scope: BaseAuction.sol, GPV2CompatibleAuction.sol, PriceManager.sol — auction lifecycle, bid validation, settlement, price feed integration
roles: ASSET_ADMIN_ROLE (Timelock), AUCTION_WORKER_ROLE (WorkflowRouter), PRICE_ADMIN_ROLE (trusted transmitter), CowSwap Solvers (external)
audit_objective: Independent security review of auction mechanics, bid validation, settlement logic, and price feed integration in the Chainlink Payment Abstraction V2 system
key_risks: Mid-auction parameter mutation, cross-auction settlement coupling, inconsistent fill path constraints, price feed timestamp assumptions, keeper performData trust
top_recommendations: Guard setMinBidUsdValue with _whenNoLiveAuctions, add upper-bound check on observationsTimestamp, re-enforce auction-end predicate in performUpkeep

findings.low:
L-01: setMinBidUsdValue() allows mutation of bid admissibility during active auctions — breaks invariant that auction parameters remain static during lifecycle.

L-02: _onAuctionEnd performs contract-global LINK settlement rather than auction-scoped — concurrent auction closures have cross-auction side effects.

L-03: isValidSignature() and bid() enforce non-equivalent minimum fill constraints — CowSwap solvers can fill orders below minBidUsdValue.

L-04: transmit() assumes monotonic observationsTimestamp but does not enforce upper bound — future timestamps silently extend price validity window.

L-05: performUpkeep() does not re-enforce the auction-end predicate before destructive settlement — treats performData as trusted input.
